meaning: vi.脱去假面具vt.撕下...的假面具,揭露,暴露
examples: What little scholarship has existed on Ernest Hemingway—considering his stature—has focused on trying to unmask the man behind the bravura.
We hide because we are afraid to unmask ourselves and let people see us for who we really are.
What the mobile ecosystems of today have done is to unmask the reality that it’s the back end that matters.
Corporations began filing so-called "John Doe" lawsuits against the anonymous posters, demanding that their hosts unmask their identities.
But that requires individual students to wage legal battles to unmask their detractors.
